ops.title: v0.12.1 (2026-07-20) tailnet legs are dual-protocol: https MagicDNS URLs (tailscale cert) + plain-http IP for mesh; printed scheme ≡ served scheme

ops.body:
v0.11.0's `http://<name>.ts.net` URLs never worked in browsers — `ts.net` is HSTS-preloaded, so browsers force-upgrade to `https://` against the plaintext leg (ERR_SSL_PROTOCOL_ERROR). Fixed in v0.12.1 (2026-07-20, with synckit v0.22.0's `MintCert`/`SelfCertDomain`): every tailnet leg sniffs each connection's first byte and serves TLS (cert for the MagicDNS name via `tailscale cert`, minted async at start, renewed on the 30s reconcile, h2 negotiates) and plain HTTP on one port. Printed URLs always match what serves: `https://<certDomain>:<port>` only while a valid cert for the live cert domain is held, else the HSTS-exempt `http://<tailnet-ip>` form; the http name URL is never composed.

Gotchas that will bite again:

- **Slugs get mangled in LLM transcription** (a relayed URL grew a stray hex char → `/events` 404; the daemon was never at fault). Since v0.12.0 the SPA shows a "Can't connect to this board" panel after ~8s instead of an eternal skeleton; a spinning board = check the slug against `/api/sessions` first.
- The primary listener (cc-interact's) is always plain TCP — with a `0.0.0.0` bind there are no sniffed legs and only IP-http URLs print. Never "fix" that into https.
- Slowloris exposure on the shared http.Server (no ReadHeaderTimeout) is a cc-interact follow-up: cc-notes backlog task 88fc433.
- `tailscale serve`/Funnel remain banned by contract — proxied requests ride the loopback path and defeat token + trust checks.
- First-ever cert issuance can take minutes; until it lands, IP-http URLs print (correct, self-heals). `CertDomains` in `tailscale status --json` gates minting — empty means the tailnet's HTTPS feature is off.
- Machines still on ≤0.11.0 (yasyf-home as of 2026-07-20) need `brew upgrade --cask cc-present` + a manual daemon stop (`pkill -f 'cc-present.* daemon'`, then any artifact command respawns) to serve https. `cc-present status` does NOT lazy-spawn.
- A bare HTTP 200 never proves a board works — probe `/api/health` for `{"version":…}`.
- synckit's flaky rpc test (`TestPostSendFailureIsNotReplayedAndNextCallReconnects`, bug 9ecf56f) — a red synckit CI on an unrelated diff is likely that; rerun before diagnosing.

Related: [[cc-present-dev-daemon-versioning]], [[cc-present-release-flow]], [[cc-present-push-dance]].
